title: Clone stormtrooper
content:
{{Top|canon=Clone trooper|unmatched=1}}
{{Organization
|image=[[Thiresenate.png]]
|name=Clone stormtrooper
|type=[[Cloning/Legends|Clone]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]<ref name="EG Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref>
|founder=
|leader=
|subgroup=
|headquarters=
|locations=
|formed=[[Clone trooper/Legends|Clone troopers]]<ref name="EG Warfare" />
|founded=[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]<ref name="EG Warfare" />
|fragmented=
|reorganized=
|dissolved=
|restored=
|affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]<ref name="BF2">''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]''</ref>
**[[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]]<ref name="EG Warfare" />
}}
{{Quote|The clone troopers, now proudly bearing the honorable rank of Imperial stormtroopers, have tackled the dangerous work of fighting our enemies on the front lines.|Palpatine|<ref name="Inaugural">{{InsiderCite|84|Republic HoloNet News Special Inaugural Edition 16:5:241}}</ref>}}
'''Clone stormtroopers''' were [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] [[Cloning/Legends|cloned]] from various [[Clone template/Legends|templates]], including the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]], whose [[DNA/Legends|genetic material]] was used in the creation of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] during the [[Great Peace of the Republic|last years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. In the aftermath of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] [[Imperialization/Legends|redesignated]] the clone [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] as Imperial stormtroopers in his [[Declaration of a New Order|newly-formed]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. In addition to the cloning operation on [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]], where the Republic's [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|clone army]] was grown, the Empire developed clone stormtroopers through its use of [[Spaarti cloning cylinder]]s belonging to [[Arkanian Microtechnologies]] as well as the [[GeNode]] program.

The [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]] became more diverse in the aftermath of a failed [[Kamino uprising|rebellion on Kamino]]; although the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] succeeded in defeating a force of [[Anti-trooper|anti-Imperial clone troopers]], Palpatine decided that the Empire would no longer rely solely on genetically-identical [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]]. Clone stormtroopers were henceforth bred from new genetic sources, reducing the Fett-based clones to a minority status in the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]]. In [[Time/Legends|time]], the Empire turned increasingly toward recruitment and conscription, using [[Reproduction/Legends|birth-born]] [[Human/Legends|Humans]] to supplement the clones in the Stormtrooper [[Corps/Legends|Corps]].

==History==
===Origins===
{{Quote|Imagine, an army of clones; the training of which you will oversee. They will be modified to grow at twice the rate of ordinary men, and will be programmed for absolute loyalty. They will be magnificent; perfect warriors, like you.|Dooku, to Jango Fett|<ref name="Bounty Hunter">''[[Star Wars: Bounty Hunter]]''</ref>}}
[[File:CoruscantGuardssearching.png|thumb|left|250px|The Coruscant Guard's clone shock troopers were also known as "stormtroopers" during the Clone Wars.]]
During the final [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], [[Count of Serenno/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] recruited the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] to serve as the [[Clone template/Legends|genetic template]] for an [[Army/Legends|army]] of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]. [[Cloning/Legends|Bred]] for combat and obedience,<ref name="Bounty Hunter" /> the clones formed the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]] that fought against the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]<ref name="EpisodeII">{{Film|II}}</ref> throughout the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="EpisodeIII">{{Film|III}}</ref> During the [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] conflict, the [[Clone shock trooper/Legends|clone shock troopers]] of the [[Coruscant Guard/Legends|Coruscant Guard]] earned the nickname "stormtroopers" in their capacity as [[Republic Military/Legends|military]] [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|enforcers]] of martial law on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. Their presence on the Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] was a visible symbol of the [[Planet/Legends|planet's]] new security measures,<ref name="RotS VD">''[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> becoming the most visible element of the [[Homeworld Security Command]].<ref name="LabEvil">''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]''</ref>

===First generation of stormtroopers===
{{Quote|Many have died in their devotion to the Empire. Imperial citizens would do well to remember their example.|Palpatine|<ref name="Inaugural">{{InsiderCite|84|Republic HoloNet News Special Inaugural Edition 16:5:241}}</ref>}}
[[File:Clone_Shock_Trooper_05.png|thumb|right|250px|After the Clone Wars, clone troopers were rechristened as Imperial stormtroopers by Emperor Palpatine.]]
The term "stormtrooper" was officially adopted by all clone [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] following the Republic's [[Declaration of a New Order|reorganization]] into the first [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] under [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]],<ref name="Inaugural" /> resulting in the troops becoming known as "clone stormtroopers."<ref name="IC501">''[[Imperial Commando: 501st]]''</ref> The newly formed [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]] served as the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] elite [[Imperial Military/Legends|military wing]] throughout the [[Imperial Era/Legends|era]] of the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]], using clones of the same template that produced the Republic's clone army.<ref name="EG Warfare" />

The Galactic Empire maintained the same army that enabled Palpatine—the public persona of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Sidious—to gain absolute power over the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] government. Though the majority of clone soldiers were still produced in the [[Tipoca City/Legends|cloning facilities]] of Kamino, the Empire also retained the services of [[Arkanian Microtechnologies]], a corporation that used [[Spaarti cloning cylinder|Spaarti technology]] to grow adult clones in a full year's time.<ref name="2EdgedSword">{{InsiderCite|85|A Two-Edged Sword}}</ref> Aside from a few outside sources,<ref name="2EdgedSword" /> the vast majority of stormtroopers were clones, engineered through [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] and [[Arkanian/Legends|Arkanian]] processes.<ref name="EG Warfare" /> 

===Enforcers of the Empire===
{{Quote|I was concerned we'd end up as glorified policemen. It's good to have a mission.|[[Vill]]|<ref name="DT4">[[Dark Times 4|''Dark Times'' 4]]</ref>}}
[[File:MassacreofNewPlympto.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Clone stormtroopers enforced the Emperor's reign during the early years of the Galactic Empire.]] 
Clone stormtroopers had been an essential factor to the Empire during its early years, and retained the [[Phase II clone trooper armor/Legends|Phase II clone trooper armor]] they customized with unit [[Color/Legends|colors]] and markings during the Clone Wars. As the Imperial code of uniformity became more strict, the stormtroopers restored their clone armor to plain white, emphasizing the Empire's preference for uniformity and obedience in contrast to individuality and creativity.<ref name="EG Warfare" /> The clones' use of nicknames, which they had developed under the encouragement of their [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi officers]], also fell out of style as [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]] referred to them only by number designations as part of the Stormtrooper Corps' doctrine of absolute loyalty and conformity to the New Order.<ref name="EG Warfare" />

Despite their new purpose as oppressors, the clones adjusted well to Imperial duties,<ref name="DT4">[[Dark Times 4|''Dark Times'' 4]]</ref> with their image as elite soldiers to be feared cemented.<ref name="DL">''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]''</ref> The clone stormtroopers were used to subjugate worlds to bring their populations into line, as well as to hunt down fugitive Jedi,<ref name="TFUvg">''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''</ref> and engaging in operations against [[Separatist holdouts/Legends|Separatist holdouts]].<ref name="DT4" />

===GeNode clones===
{{Quote|How can they not know they are clones?|[[Milo Strander]]|<ref name="PaxEmpirica">{{StoryCite|story=Pax Empirica—The Wookiee Annihilation|book=Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide}}</ref>}}
[[File:Stormtrooper-CotF.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The GeNode program produced genetically-engineered stormtroopers who were unaware that they were clones.]]
A modified version of the Arkanian cloning process was developed during the early years of the New Order, enabling the Empire to initiate the [[GeNode]] program that created clone lines of soldiers that were noted for efficiency and obedience. But unlike the clones that were created through Kaminoan and Arkanian techniques, GeNode clones were conditioned to believe that they were real human beings,<ref name="EG Warfare" /> a falsehood reinforced by artificial memories and full names.<ref name="PaxEmpirica" /> As a result of their programming, they could not comprehend the fact that they were clones, nor were they able to discuss cloning with other GeNode clones.<ref name="EG Warfare" />

GeNode clone lines were maintained in clandestine facilities located in the Empire's [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. The program used at least four genotypes, although GeNode subjects were conditioned with the inability to recognize fellow clones of the same template. A unit of GeNode stormtroopers participated in an [[Battle of Kashyyyk (19 BBY)|Imperial invasion]] of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], where they served under the command of the Emperor's [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. The GeNode program ultimately fell out of favor due to cases of psychological [[Clone madness|instability]] in its clone subjects. Despite all efforts to prevent the clones from discovering their true origins, some GeNodes overcame their programming and were horrified to find themselves surrounded by identical soldiers, leading to several occasions where defective [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] [[Death/Legends|killed]] their fellow clones in rage and terror.<ref name="EG Warfare" />

===Clone rebellion and consequence===
{{Quote|After the Kamino uprising, the Emperor decided that an army of genetically identical soldiers was too susceptible to corruption. Future troopers would be cloned from a variety of templates. Though the 501st itself remained pure, the rest of the Imperial Army gradually became more and more diverse. We never really got used to the new guys.|[[Retired clone trooper]]|<ref name="BF2" />}}
[[File:501st_Trooper_TBOH.png|thumb|left|250px|The clone stormtroopers' ranks became varied through the use of new genetic sources, turning Jango Fett's clones into a minority in the Imperial Military.]]
While making up the vast majority of the early stormtrooper ranks, in 12 BBY, a [[Kamino uprising|rebellion]] on Kamino headed by disgruntled Kaminoan cloners exposed a secret army of clones that had been engineered to fight against the Galactic Empire. Though the Kaminoans and their army of rogue clones were annihilated and the uprising neutralized, the incident convinced Palpatine that the Empire could no longer depend on an army of clone troopers comprised entirely from the same genotype. A vast variety of genetic templates were established soon after the Kaminoan rebels' defeat, leading to the production of soldiers from various non-Fett templates.<ref name="BF2" /> While the Fett clones remained a dominant force within the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]], the rest of their brethren were outnumbered by non-Fett clones whom they referred to as the "new guys."<ref name="BF2" />

===Decline and replacement===
{{Quote|Since the Empire has redirected the clone trooper program to other pursuits and stepped up recruiting inferior humans from the Outer Rim, the operational effectiveness of this army has declined significantly.|Cody|<ref name="TFUII">''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II]]''</ref>}}
[[File:Recruiting_poster_Empire_EGTW.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Clones were supplemented by birth-born stormtroopers after the Empire turned to recruitment and conscription to bolster its forces.]]
The Galactic Empire had banned the creation of more clone soldiers while also keeping its remaining cloning operations in full production, with these locations of all facilities throughout the Empire were classified and placed under military jurisdiction for security purposes.<ref name="PaxEmpirica" /> However, as the significance of clone stormtroopers steadily declined over the course of several years, enlisted stormtroopers continued to fill this void instead.<ref name="EG Warfare" /> Although Emperor Palpatine and Darth Vader both personally favored clones as soldiers,<ref name="2EdgedSword" /> the Empire began to gradually supplement its clone units with birth-born recruits due to the strain that cloning had on the economy and its military. The Kaminoan process created an army that had been hailed during the Clone Wars as the greatest fighting force in [[Timeline of galactic history/Legends|galactic history]], but their clones were an expensive drain on financial resources and took a decade to mature.<ref name="EG Warfare" /> Arkanian technology was meant to compensate for the long growth process of Kaminoan clones,<ref name="IHI">{{StoryCite|story=In His Image|book=Vader: The Ultimate Guide}}</ref> but their products were well below the efficiency rate that had been credited to the Grand Army of the Republic.<ref name="EG Warfare" /> 

With the drawbacks of the Imperial Military's dependence on cloning, the Empire had no such shortage of [[Human/Legends|Humans]] that could be recruited to serve as stormtroopers. The number of enlisted troopers increased exponentially throughout the decade before the Battle of Yavin, culminating in their rise as the dominant majority in the ranks while clones became a rarity<ref name="EG Warfare" /> by [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]].<ref name="TFUII" /><ref name="TFUIIc">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II (graphic novel)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II'' graphic novel]]</ref> Commander [[Cody/Legends|Cody]], a veteran of the Clone Wars, lamented the state of the Imperial Military by the time he was stationed on Kamino during the Imperial Era. Tasked with overseeing the Imperial garrison on the Kaminoan homeworld, he viewed the non-clone Human stormtroopers under his command as incompetent and inferior to clone troopers.<ref name="TFUII" />

===Thrawn's clone army===
{{Quote|With Mount Tantiss and the Emperor's collection of Spaarti cloning cylinders, the initiative is once again ours. Very soon, we'll begin taking planets back from the Rebellion… For that, we will need a well-trained army.|Thrawn, to [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]]|<ref name="DFR">''[[Dark Force Rising]]''</ref>}}
The deaths of Emperor Palpatine and Darth Vader in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] left the Galactic Empire in a state of utter [[Imperial Civil War|chaos and disorder]] following the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance's]] victory at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Without a clear line of succession, the central government on Coruscant failed to prevent the Stormtrooper Corps from becoming fragmented into smaller units under the control of various [[Warlord/Legends|warlords]] who squabbled over leadership of the Empire.<ref name="EG Warfare" /> By [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], five years after the Imperials' defeat on [[Endor/Legends|Endor]], [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] launched a [[Thrawn campaign|campaign]] to reunite the fractured Imperial remnants under his own leadership by destroying the rebels—now reconstituted as the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="DFR" />

[[File:KatanaClones-DFR6.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Grand Admiral Thrawn attempted to create a new generation of clone stormtroopers during his conflict with the New Republic.]]
In order to realize his dream of a resurgent empire, Thrawn replenished the Stormtrooper Corps with a [[Thrawn's army|new army of clone soldiers]] engineered via several Spaarti cylinders. The clones were bred from a variety of templates, including [[General/Legends|General]] [[Maximilian Veers/Legends|Maximilian Veers]], [[Baron of the Empire/Legends|Baron]] [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]], [[Corporal/Legends|Corporal]] [[Terkuss]] of the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] and [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Creb]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. In addition, the stormtrooper clones were cut off from the Force via the use of [[Ysalamiri/Legends|ysalamiri]], resulting in the production of clones in a matter of weeks, putting them at an advantage.<ref name="Author's Cut 7">{{SW|url=news/the-essential-guide-to-warfare-authors-cut-part-7-the-grand-army-of-the-republic|series=Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|text=Part 7 — The Grand Army of the Republic}}</ref> The creation of clone stormtroopers rejuvenated the Empire's depleted manpower, but the Imperial counteroffensive against the Republic was short-lived due to the assassination of Thrawn by his own [[Noghri/Legends|Noghri]] bodyguards.<ref name="DFR" />

===Legacy===
{{Quote|My old man was a trooper. Member of the 407th. Always talked about {{'}}''the honor of the armor''{{'}}.|[[Anson Trask]]|<ref name="Legacy (2006) 4">[[Legacy (2006) 4|''Legacy'' (2006) 4]]</ref>}}
[[File:Jokers.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The new Galactic Empire made use of enlisted stormtroopers, Humans and aliens alike, instead of clones.]]
After the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] ended in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]] with the decisive victory of the New Republic, the Stormtrooper Corps survived the dissolution of Palpatine's New Order, but as a remnant of its former glory. Stormtroopers still existed throughout the decades after the Battle of Endor. However, the [[Empire of the Hand]], the [[Imperial Remnant]] and various other successor states of the original Empire became heavily reliant on the recruitment of enlisted troopers. Under Palpatine's reign, stormtrooper recruits and clones were exclusively Human.<ref name="EG Warfare" /> The reforms introduced by the Hand and Remnant governments allowed [[Alien/Legends|Non-Humans]] to join the Stormtrooper Corps.<ref name="SQ">''[[Survivor's Quest]]''</ref> By [[130 ABY]], clone stormtroopers were virtually unheard of as the military became heavily diverse in a sea of enlisted troopers from a variety of different backgrounds.<ref name="EG Warfare" /><ref name="Legacy0">[[Star Wars: Legacy (2006) 0½|''Star Wars: Legacy'' (2006) 0½]]</ref>

==Equipment==
{{Quote|Armed with deadly new weapons, blazing new ships, and shiny new armor, our presence let the galaxy know that the days of the Old Republic were well and truly over.|Retired clone trooper|<ref name="BF2" />}}
[[File:First_stromtroopers_EGT_Warfare.png|thumb|right|250px|Clone stormtroopers wore Phase II clone trooper armor until the development of the Imperial stormtrooper armor.]]
Retaining their Phase II clone trooper armor, clone stormtroopers initially retained custom insignias, colors, and attachments on their armor. As the Imperial Era dragged on, the clones eventually restored their armor to its original plain [[Color/Legends|white]] plates, emphasizing a preference for uniformity and obedience in contrast to individuality and creativity. The standard-issue weapons of clone stormtroopers was the [[DC-15S/Legends|DC-15S]] and the [[DC-15A blaster rifle/Legends|DC-15A blaster rifle]]. Clone stormtroopers would eventually transition into standard stormtrooper armor as the Imperial Era dragged on.<ref name="EG Warfare" />
